mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with t-stat > 2.0, or rolling Rank IC exceeds 0.05 negatively.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack reduces wage inflation pressures and signals impending economic slowdown, causing equity markets to price in lower corporate profit growth and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand and residential investment, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 or positive returns occurring in > 70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production signal immediate supply chain bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with INDPRO contractions, or the signal reverses to positive during recovery phases with p-value < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPI levels above 250 indicate entrenched inflation that forces monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative mean during periods of payroll growth, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer momentum despite aggregate output stability, prompting equity sell-offs as profit margins face downward pressure over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, with out-of-sample classification accuracy below 50% or positive returns dominating during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 100 while unemployment remains under 4%, tight labor markets constrain output expansion, triggering short-term equity volatility and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances, or the joint condition shows no statistical edge over baseline market returns.
